input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

It transitions to reveal a logo featuring "Rubio" in black cursive script above "MONOCOAT" in bold, black sans-serif capitals. Below, smaller text reads "INFINITY FOR WOOD". A small dash appears above the script. The background remains white throughout.

samples.output: <think>Below the bold "MONOCOAT" text, the subtitle changes. The previous phrase is replaced by the URL "RUBIOMONOCOATUSA.COM" in light grey, uppercase letters. The upper script logo, dash, and bold brand name remain fixed against the plain white background throughout this duration.</think><action>silent</action>

[10-14] The video cuts to the person using a black orbital sander attached to a green vacuum hose. The label "PREP" appears, followed by the text: "Sand according to industry guidelines. We recommend sanding to 120 grit."

[14-16] A person uses a black orbital sander connected to a green hose on a light wood table. The text overlay reads "Sand according to industry guidelines. We recommend sanding to 120 grit." with a "PREP" label visible.
[16-22] The tool changes to a black vacuum attachment on the green hose. The person vacuums the table surface thoroughly. The text updates to "Vacuum the surface." while the "PREP" label persists.
[22-28] The person, wearing a grey shirt, blue jeans, and black gloves, transitions to cleaning. They hold a green spray can and a white cloth. The text changes to "Clean the surface using Raw Wood Cleaner". They spray the cloth and wipe the table. Eventually, the green can is placed on the table while wiping continues.

[28-38] A person wearing black gloves wipes a light wood table with a white cloth, causing a darkened wet region to expand across the grain. A green spray can remains stationary on the table. The text "Clean the surface using Raw Wood Cleaner" and "PREP" is displayed at the bottom. [38-42] The bottom text changes to "Once finished, allow to dry completely" and a "TIP: Use moisture meter" overlay appears. The person continues wiping the surface, eventually holding the green spray can in their left hand while wiping with the right.

[48-52] A person wearing black gloves stands at a metal wire rack, pouring a clear liquid from a small green can into a plastic cup containing dark liquid. They then stir the resulting dark grey mixture with a wooden stick. Text at the bottom reads "APPLY OP2C CLEAN UP" and instructs: "Mix Part A & Part B at a ratio of 3:1 (A:B) Once mixed, pot life is 6 hours." [52-56] The scene shifts to a large light wooden table where the person pours the dark oil in a continuous line down the center. Holding a brown applicator pad in their other hand, they prepare to spread the liquid. The on-screen text changes to: "Apply the oil to the surface working in sections that allow you to apply the oil and remove the excess within 10-15 minutes."

[56-64] A person wearing a grey shirt, blue jeans, and black gloves uses a brown applicator pad to spread dark oil onto a light wooden table. Initially, they spread a long, dark line of oil poured down the center. They work the applicator back and forth, spreading the oil across the surface, creating a widening dark, wet patch that covers more of the wood grain. The on-screen text instructs: "Apply the oil to the surface working in sections that allow you to apply the oil and remove the excess within 10-15 minutes." The person continues to rub the oil into the wood, expanding the treated area. Text in the bottom left corner reads "APPLY OP2C CLEAN UP".

[44-46] The scene shifts to a person standing behind a metal wire rack, pouring dark liquid from a green can into a clear plastic cup with a wooden stir stick. A second green can sits on the rack. Bottom text displays "APPLY OP2C" and mixing instructions for Part A and Part B at a 3:1 ratio.

[46-48] A person wearing black gloves continues to pour a dark substance from a green can into a clear plastic cup situated on a metal rack. A wooden stir stick is visible inside the cup. The caption at the bottom remains, instructing to mix Part A and Part B at a 3:1 ratio with a 6-hour pot life.

[64-66] A person wearing a grey shirt and black gloves uses a brown applicator pad to spread dark oil across a light wooden table surface. The wet, darkened region expands as the tool moves further down the table. The caption at the bottom remains static, instructing on application timing and section work.

[66-68] A person wearing black gloves continues spreading dark oil on a light wooden table using a brown applicator pad. The darkened, treated section of the wood grows larger. The caption at the bottom changes to state: "Oil Plus 2C will form a molecular bond with the wood fibers in 3-5 minutes.

[68-70] A person in a grey shirt and black gloves spreads dark oil on a light wood table using a white applicator. The bottom caption regarding molecular bonding remains visible as the treated area expands across the surface.

[52-56] The scene shifts to a large light wooden table where the person pours the dark oil in a continuous line down the center. Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

The text "APPLY OP2C CLEAN UP" is displayed in the bottom left corner. A caption at the bottom reads: "If necessary, continue to the next section and repeat steps until the surface is finished."

A person in a grey shirt and blue jeans kneels at the far end of a wooden table, rubbing the surface with a green-edged applicator pad. A cup with dark liquid and a white cloth sit nearby. input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
